title: Cleartext Industrial Credentials in PCAP-Derived Metadata

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 4.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 4.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.31: Cleartext Industrial Credentials in PCAP-Derived Metadata.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.31' running on claroty:alert or nozomi:packets_meta (example sourcetype), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=ot_sec (sourcetype="claroty:alert" OR sourcetype="nozomi:alerts") earliest=-7d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)password=|passwd=|user=.*pass") AND NOT match(_raw,"(?i)\\*\\*\\*")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<proto>modbus|opcua|dnp3).*"
| stats count by proto, src_ip, dest_ip, signature
| sort - count
description: Scans IDS metadata for username or password fields in cleartext industrial protocols where confidentiality must be preserved.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_sec and sourcetypes claroty:alert, nozomi:alerts. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Never index full payload in prod—use IDS summaries; (2) rotate exposed creds; (3) enable TLS where vendor supports; (4) segment sniffers. Indexes required: `index=ot_sec`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=claroty:alert`, `sourcetype=nozomi:alerts`.
visualization: Table, Single value (hits), Bar by protocol.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Claroty xDome TA, Nozomi TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `claroty:alert` or `nozomi:packets_meta` (example sourcetype).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Never index full payload in prod—use IDS summaries; (2) rotate exposed creds; (3) enable TLS where vendor supports; (4) segment sniffers.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_sec (sourcetype="claroty:alert" OR sourcetype="nozomi:alerts") earliest=-7d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)password=|passwd=|user=.*pass") AND NOT match(_raw,"(?i)\\*\\*\\*")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<proto>modbus|opcua|dnp3).*"
| stats count by proto, src_ip, dest_ip, signature
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cleartext Industrial Credentials in PCAP-Derived Metadata (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 4.1)** — Scans IDS metadata for username or password fields in cleartext industrial protocols where confidentiality must be preserved.

Documented **Data sources**: `claroty:alert` or `nozomi:packets_meta` (example sourcetype). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Claroty xDome TA, Nozomi TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_sec; **sourcetype**: claroty:alert, nozomi:alerts. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_sec, sourcetype="claroty:alert", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(_raw,"(?i)password=|passwd=|user=.*pass") AND NOT match(_raw,"(?i)\\*\\*\\*")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by proto, src_ip, dest_ip, signature** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cyber Vision / Nozomi (or your industrial IDS console) and the same traffic slice. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value (hits), Bar by protocol.
grandmaExplanation: We scans IDS metadata for username or password fields in cleartext industrial protocols where confidentiality must be preserved. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
